hey so i recently got dafodil but she eat aritmetica like crazy any wheel or character that can aid that ?

Uh, kind of. There are only a few wheels in the game that help with arith. If you run titan's blade on daff that can discount her cards when she exalts. I have seen a fair amount of daffs with titans blade. Lots of different dps really like that wheel.

The other wheel is amidst the downpour (lighthouse) which gives you +1 arith on the first strike you play each turn. I like using amidst the downpour on a support like clem with the twisted twins black cov so that I can trigger her quasar without having to lose out on arith. That can help leave you with more resources for your dps to work with.

what character do i pick from the free reroll banner?
If you follow Flares' advice and go blue realm route, then imo you should get nautilia (Chaos/Yellow realm). She provides you good survivability early game and she also gives blue realm good support.
After you get Nautilia, go all out on blue realm standard summons (don't use silver for it)
I don't know people saying Jenkins too
Winkle also
After you get e3 caecus and e2 goliath, you will be stuck in wheel (artifact) jail for a loooong time.

Of course, those are also strong option, but flares' recommendation is all about speedrunning early game to reach end game asap, and blue realm is the easiest way to do that. 

If you pick Jenkins or Winkles, you can't go blue realm.
Should he pick Jenkins or Wrinkles?
Can he mix and match purple and blue realm?
Reroll banner literally doesn't matter , because you get so many pure cores from playing the story
You can :
1. Pick caec, and go blue like flare recommends
2. Pick what you like
3. Pick helot for the speed run comp
Ok

Is clem the best Ultra support? Debatable with Horla, but both are definitely up there.
The 'issue' with Clem is that shes most often seen Over Exalted in very high end teams, as a unique form of damage boost for one shot shenanigans. But she's fine to use in less invested teams, as an all rounder (buffs, heals, ennemy debuffs, side of damage) support

I'm lost, what's wheel jail?
It's when you invest your standard cores into standard wheels instead of awakeners and what a lot of people do  for most of their accounts runtime
+12 wheels are very powerful because you can equip a second SSR wheel with them
and most people will only ever get standard wheels to +12
Titan's Blade +12 is the important one and afterwards you most likely know what you want to pull next on.

Im not quite sure who’s best with who, but I believe my best characters rn is murphy:fx and tulu<:please:1356053110375714817> <:please:1356053110375714817> I just need tips on teams that work well and good builds because with all the characters on my team the card thingies I have on them are the ones meant for them

by ch2 you mean... the wax museum? yeah nah you barely started

early on the meta is weird, enemies have no hp, and team comp doesnt really matter. the advice i give to newbies at this stage is just try different characters, take the opportunity to learn what each of them does, try out different realms

Tentacles get a lot worse at endgame because enemy hp scales faster than tentacle damage. Defensive units like celeste get better because you are actually in danger of dying in 1-2 turns if you dont plan ahead

Once you reach a stage where its too difficult to keep going, you can ask again, this time you will have more experience and have a better idea of what works and what doesnt

The strongest character on your team is murphy: fauxborn (who we call gmurphy, as in genesis alter murphy)

she is a broken support tank dps who does everything

she works well with defensive units that help you not die to sacrifice; characters that feed her aliemus so she can exalt more; and characters that can find her waltz card or copy/doublecast it.

in lategame there are passive bonuses for running units with the Lemurian trait together, so her endgame team is miryam tulu goliath/faros (but it ONLY works in endgame, it sucks early on)

uvhash is ok, he's a self damaging carry like gmurphy except he does less damage and needs more support

tulu and celeste are situational support characters for blue teams
Gmurphy, sponsored by KaDe bot.

He isn't really self centered. His only job is to steal STR (E2) and generate STR with his exalt.
Plus a stat stick <:cute1:1356053053568192717>
there is no "tentacle synergy" with faros, 2 tentacle hits and a few hundred poison are a rounding error on your damage
faros is run because of her exalt that draws 3 cards and makes a shield
goliath is run because his str steal enables huge burst damage turns

doesn't her DMG amp work on tentacles too?
or it is negligible compared to STR?
its negligible compared to str
dmg amp is only applied to base dmg so if u get buffs in battle (via str, or gmurphy 3 cost increasing tent dmg) it doesnt get affected by it anymore thats why its mostly not super useful

200 more tentacle dmg

Hey yall, cleared the last chapter of Arc 1 only needing to burn one Gnosis, feeling like Arc 2 chapter 1 is a real difficulty spike but hearing other folks say this team should be even stronger now. Are my covenants messing me up? Is it my talent investment? Skill issue? 🥲

Arc 2 is a difficulty spike, that's true. Gmurphy teams sustain by relying on her talent that grants shields when lemurians exalt. You also need to make good use of miryam's rouse as a weakness/vulnerable source.
Investment into your team will help a lot. Soul forge up to 3-5 is important for your supports
Gmurphy's personal stats are also important, so more levels for her
I’ve heard some folks suggest swapping vanilla Murphy for Farros because the lemurian buff is so strong, but I feel like that would make me struggle with shield and managing gMurphys DoT. Thoughts?
Also try to get the dream of medicine covenant for her, I don't think steppenwolf does that much for her
